title: CSV import silently duplicates rows on retry
bug_description: Retrying a failed CSV import creates duplicate rows instead of treating the retry as idempotent.
expected_behavior: A retried import should either skip already persisted rows or safely upsert them without duplication.
actual_behavior: The retry path inserts duplicate rows and downstream totals become inflated.

preconditions: Local import worker is running with a test CSV fixture
The fixture contains at least one row that will trigger a retryable failure on the first run
steps: Start the CSV import job with the retryable fixture
Allow the first attempt to fail after partial persistence
Trigger the retry path and inspect the imported row count
reproduction.expected_behavior: After retry, each logical CSV row should appear exactly once.
reproduction.actual_behavior: Rows already inserted on the first attempt are inserted again on retry.

logs: Import worker log shows the same row key persisted twice across first attempt and retry

manual_checks: Re-run the import with the same fixture and confirm row counts stay stable after retry
Confirm downstream totals match the unique-row expectation

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if successful imports begin skipping valid new rows.
rollback.notes: Keep the last passing import patch or branch ready for restore.

scope_notes: Keep the fix inside import deduplication, retry handling, and the directly related tests. Do not redesign the full ingestion pipeline.

notes.root_cause_hint: The retry path may be missing a persisted-row identity check or idempotency token reuse.
notes.risk_hint: An over-broad fix could skip legitimate new rows or break bulk-import throughput.
similar_bugs: idempotency key ignored on worker retry
